While I don't have specific texts by him here, the Bahá'í writings offer profound insights into unity and guidance. Shoghi Effendi speaks of a ["Plan of Unified Action"](https://oceanlibrary.com/bahai-administration_shoghi-effendi) in *Bahá’í Administration*, emphasizing the importance of collective effort. In the realm of Islam, the Qur'án describes how a ["clear Book"](https://oceanlibrary.com/sura-v-the-table_muhammad/?paraId=para_15) guides believers from darkness to light, *Sura V. The Table*. The Jewish tradition offers comfort through the words of Isaiah: ["Comfort ye, comfort ye My people"](https://oceanlibrary.com/jps-book-of-isaiah/?paraId=para_741), *Book of Isaiah*. In Christianity, the Gospel of John begins with the profound statement that ["In the beginning was the Word"](https://oceanlibrary.com/kjv-gospel-of-john/?paraId=para_8), highlighting the divine nature of Christ. Meanwhile, Buddhist texts, such as the *Sutra Collection*, offer insights into the path of enlightenment. Each tradition provides a unique perspective on spiritual growth and understanding.
